## 48. Docusign rapidly scales its AI strategy by enabling its global workforce with Gemini

**Source:** https://workspace.google.com/blog/customer-stories/docusign-rapidly-scales-its-ai-strategy-by-enabling-its-global-workforce-with-gemini?hl=en

# Docusign rapidly scales its AI strategy by enabling its global workforce with Gemini

Source: https://workspace.google.com/blog/customer-stories/docusign-rapidly-scales-its-ai-strategy-by-enabling-its-global-workforce-with-gemini?hl=en

Customer Stories

# Docusign rapidly scales its AI strategy by enabling its global workforce with Gemini

March 25, 2026

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Hero_1.max-2500x2500.jpg](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Hero_1.max-2500x2500.jpg)

##### Shivi Singh Verma

Senior Manager, Engineering (Cloud Apps), Docusign

##### Try Google Workspace at No Cost

Get a business email, all the storage you need, video conferencing, and more.

[SIGN UP](https://workspace.google.com/business/signup/welcome?source=gafb-blog_article-body-en)

**NotebookLM is a key differentiator for us. Because its knowledge base is built on our own unique data, everyone at Docusign can easily find any information they need.**

**Snapshot:** As a pioneer in electronic signatures, Docusign has always operated with a technology-first mindset. In its search for innovation, it became an early adopter of Gemini and today relies on Google Workspace with Gemini to power its global team’s productivity and collaboration.

- **90% reduction in time** spent on performance review preparation with NotebookLM
- Saves **1-4 hours** per employee per week
- 80% of employees feel **Gemini has a positive impact** on their day-to-day work
- Automated support chat for new policies with **NotebookLM and Gemini-based chatbots**
- Switched from **Office365 to Google Workspace** to give Docusign a more modern, intuitive, and collaborative working experience

## **Origin story**

From its inception, [Docusign](https://www.docusign.com/) has helped lead efforts to standardize and redefine the perception of electronic signatures. Our teams do this by committing themselves to using new technologies to shape internal operations and develop our products for more than 1.7 million customers. Most recently, that means dismantling barriers to AI and weaving it into our daily workflows. We see our company’s collaboration solutions as the starting place for our teams’ innovation, but that wasn’t always the case.

Our previous collaboration solutions were disconnected and software sprawl made it challenging for everyone to work together. Our systems of record and everyday work tools were disconnected, forcing employees to spend valuable time searching for information before they could act. We knew there had to be more efficient ways to connect our global teams and deliver faster by offering better-connected and more user-friendly tools.

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Google_Workspace_Inline_Image_1_gvyrFzv.max-2000x2000.jpg](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Google_Workspace_Inline_Image_1_gvyrFzv.max-2000x2000.jpg)

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Google_Workspace_Inline_Image_1_gvyrFzv.max-2000x2000.jpg](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Google_Workspace_Inline_Image_1_gvyrFzv.max-2000x2000.jpg)

By building a list of “gold standard” Gemini responses, Docusign works to continually refine and hone prompting to make every team as efficient as possible.

## **The switch to Google Workspace and NotebookLM**

We chose to move from Office365 to Google Workspace to give Docusign a more modern, intuitive, and collaborative working experience. It was especially important for us to work in a cloud-first environment that reflected how our teams already operated, including native integration with our other apps. Now, we trust Google Drive as our central repository for all of our data while communicating through Gmail, collaborating in real time through Docs and Slides, and even collecting internal data with Forms. The real evolutionary leap came when we adopted [Gemini](https://cloud.google.com/ai/gemini-for-work?hl=en), giving our teams access to generative AI directly within the apps they already use every day.

After consulting with other enterprise users, we saw that Gemini’s feature set was growing, and it supported our ability to scale AI quickly. It also meets the same enterprise security standards as the rest of Google Workspace, allowing us to confidently integrate AI while maintaining our zero-trust architecture and access controls. Our implementation team then helped introduce our entire org to the ways that AI could fit uniquely into our workflows. They educated us, for example, on the best practices for prompting.

Investing early in training helped accelerate responsible AI adoption across the organization. We used Forms to collect feedback from our teams. That survey found 80% of participants reported a positive impact on their work lives and 67% saved 1-4 hours of time each week, boosting their productivity. With that time back, everyone at Docusign can focus their attention on higher impact, strategic work to grow the company. As a result, Gemini is now a critical tool for us on new projects, data analysis, and employee learning.

[NotebookLM](https://notebooklm.google/) has also become a powerful knowledge and research assistant for our teams. Because its knowledge base is built on our own unique data, everyone at Docusign can easily find any information they need, and customize it for their format preferences. I can use it to generate infographics for updates to leadership just as easily as I can craft more tailored, comprehensive performance reviews for my team. Conducting quarterly performance reviews used to take me four to five hours per person. With unique notebooks, I can query existing notes, build a report, and be ready to meet with them in less than 30 minutes. As thousands of employees at Docusign embrace this paradigm shift, we’ll only continue to innovate and drive insights faster.

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/GoogleWorkspaceInlineImage2.max-1500x1500.jpg](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/GoogleWorkspaceInlineImage2.max-1500x1500.jpg)

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/GoogleWorkspaceInlineImage2.max-1500x1500.jpg](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/GoogleWorkspaceInlineImage2.max-1500x1500.jpg)

With Gemini and NotebookLM embedded into day-to-day work at Docusign, every team member is helping build an AI-first culture.

## **Looking ahead**

NotebookLM has already had such an impact at both the individual and the organizational level by introducing more automation and broadening access to our institutional knowledge. We continue to refine these processes by evaluating prompt accuracy against “gold standard” reference answers to ensure we’re getting the most out of each request.

We want to continue to support our teams through solutions like [Gemini Enterprise](https://cloud.google.com/gemini-enterprise). Our vision is to deploy agents that serve as a single point of entry for groups of employees at Docusign. This may look like an HR agent that supports new hires with questions about onboarding or agents that support our product engineers by surfacing product documentation. This ongoing work to improve access will help all of us drive innovation, collaboration, and productivity across the globe.

## 49. Duet AI for Google Workspace Enterprise — now available for preorder

**Source:** https://workspace.google.com/blog/product-announcements/duet-ai-now-available-preorder?hl=en

# Duet AI for Google Workspace Enterprise — now available for preorder

Source: https://workspace.google.com/blog/product-announcements/duet-ai-now-available-preorder?hl=en

Product Announcements

# Duet AI for Google Workspace Enterprise — now available for preorder

June 8, 2023

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Bay_View_Blog_post_2436_x_1200_2.max-2500x2500.jpg](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Bay_View_Blog_post_2436_x_1200_2.max-2500x2500.jpg)

##### Steve Holt

Vice President, Strategy, Google Workspace

##### Google Workspace Newsletter

Keep up with the evolving future of work and collaboration with insights, trends, and product news.

[SIGN UP](https://workspace.google.com/?source=gafb-blog_article-body-en#newsletter)

**Editorial note: Duet AI for Google Workspace is now Gemini for Google Workspace. [Learn more](https://workspace.google.com/blog/product-announcements/gemini-for-google-workspace).**

---

At this year’s Google I/O, [we introduced Duet AI for Google Workspace](https://workspace.google.com/blog/product-announcements/duet-ai), a powerful new way of working that embeds the power of generative AI across Workspace. Duet AI helps you write, visualize, organize, accelerate workflows, have richer meetings, and much more. We’ve received a lot of helpful feedback from the thousands of organizations and hundreds of thousands of users enrolled in Workspace Labs. We’ve incorporated this feedback into a new offering we’re launching today, **Duet AI for Google Workspace Enterprise**, which is now available for pre-order and will be generally available in all regions where Workspace is sold later this year.

Duet AI for Google Workspace Enterprise enables enterprise organizations to create, connect, and collaborate like never before:

- Helping you write and refine content in Gmail and Google Docs
- Creating original images from text, right within Google Slides
- Turning ideas into action and data into insights with Google Sheets
- Helping to foster more meaningful connections in Google Meet
- Keeping you in the flow of work with AI building blocks in Docs

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/original_images/Slides_blog_XwrrgNN.gif](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/original_images/Slides_blog_XwrrgNN.gif)

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/original_images/Slides_blog_XwrrgNN.gif](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/original_images/Slides_blog_XwrrgNN.gif)

Now, you can generate a bespoke image for a Slides presentation with the help of Duet AI.

If you’re interested in preordering Duet AI for Google Workspace Enterprise, please fill out this [form](https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSdWQ5IsIV4TPLDcpQzNsAzTIJ0xtFpKZUNqx6HKyYxWB2gWgg/viewform?resourcekey=0-q4ErLfPtvgtmHUWSN1lEIQ). We’ll have more information to share on availability for small business, education, and consumer users in the coming months.

## 50. Enterprise security controls for Google Workspace with Gemini

**Source:** https://workspace.google.com/blog/ai-and-machine-learning/enterprise-security-controls-google-workspace-gemini?hl=en

# Enterprise security controls for Google Workspace with Gemini

Source: https://workspace.google.com/blog/ai-and-machine-learning/enterprise-security-controls-google-workspace-gemini?hl=en

AI and Machine Learning

# Enterprise security controls for Google Workspace with Gemini

July 17, 2025

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/unnamed_3_2AuFC1M.max-1600x1600.png](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/unnamed_3_2AuFC1M.max-1600x1600.png)

##### Stephanie Chan

Security Product Marketing Manager

##### Adam Gavish

Senior Product Manager, Google Workspace

##### Google Workspace Newsletter

Keep up with the evolving future of work and collaboration with insights, trends, and product news.

[SIGN UP](https://workspace.google.com/?source=gafb-blog_article-body-en#newsletter)

*“Security was at the forefront of our enterprise’s most critical needs as we looked to implement a generative AI solution. During our search, we ran a platform ‘bake-off’ and found that Gemini met all our security needs. The solution inherits all our existing Workspace security settings to keep our data, prompts, and work within the tenant, giving us peace of mind to deploy generative AI across our enterprise safely.”***- JK Krug, Vice President of Digital Employee Experience, Equifax**

Generative AI has woven itself into the fabric of users’ daily lives, becoming an indispensable tool for productivity, innovation, and convenience. With its rapid adoption and growing importance, organizations are rightly concerned about potential data security and privacy risks. Evaluating generative AI’s infrastructure, data governance policies, and security controls to protect sensitive data is a must.

Google Workspace with Gemini is enterprise-ready. First, Gemini keeps customer data [confidential](https://support.google.com/a/answer/15706919) and can support compliance with different regulatory frameworks, such as HIPAA and FedRAMP High. Second, Gemini is built with a [layered defense strategy for prompt injection mitigation](https://security.googleblog.com/2025/06/mitigating-prompt-injection-attacks.html), an emerging attack vector against AI systems. And third, Workspace comes with granular user access and data security controls, helping administrators safely and securely deploy AI tools, such as the Gemini app, Gemini in Workspace apps, and NotebookLM, across their organizations with confidence. Let’s take a closer look at these controls.

### Protecting your data

Protecting sensitive data is paramount. Gemini integrates with existing data security measures in Workspace to help prevent unauthorized access and exfiltration.

- [Create and manage trust rules for Drive sharing](https://support.google.com/a/answer/10621317): Trust rules in Drive help restrict Gemini's access by controlling how data is shared between internal and external users. Since Gemini can only retrieve data the user has access to, these rules can limit what Gemini is able to retrieve.
- [Understand Gemini’s data access with Drive inventory reporting](https://support.google.com/a/answer/15141054?hl=en): Admins can utilize Drive inventory reporting to gain a holistic view of how their data is classified, who can access it, and how it’s being used.
- [Apply IRM controls to restrict Gemini’s access to sensitive data](https://support.google.com/a/answer/9656855?product_name=UnuFlow&sjid=16230191034105272886-NA&visit_id=638762643105374618-2259026318&rd=1&src=supportwidget0#irmfaq&zippy=%2Cdoes-gemini-respect-dlp-irm-policies): Data loss prevention (DLP) policies can apply information rights management (IRM) controls to sensitive files. When IRM is applied (e.g., preventing download, printing, or copying), Gemini does not retrieve those protected files to generate a response.
- [Apply client-side encryption to prevent Gemini’s access to sensitive data](https://support.google.com/a/answer/10741897): For the highest level of data protection, client-side encryption (CSE) can be used. When CSE is enabled, the protected data is indecipherable to any unauthorized third-party, including Google or any generative AI assistants, such as Gemini.

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Screenshot_2025-07-11_10.21.44_AM.max-2000x2000.png](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Screenshot_2025-07-11_10.21.44_AM.max-2000x2000.png)

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Screenshot_2025-07-11_10.21.44_AM.max-2000x2000.png](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Screenshot_2025-07-11_10.21.44_AM.max-2000x2000.png)

Admin console UI for data loss prevention with a rule configuration to “disable download, print, and copy” in Google Drive for all collaborators.

### Understanding AI usage

Transparency and accountability are crucial for security. Workspace provides comprehensive logging and exporting capabilities for Gemini activity.

- [Review Gemini usage and data access in your organization](https://support.google.com/a/answer/14564320?hl=en&src=supportwidget0&authuser=0): Admins are able to review Gemini usage and data access across their organization to understand Gemini adoption. These reports provide the overall usage level in the Gemini app and Gemini in Workspace, as well as adoption of Gemini on a per-app basis.
- Query, investigate, and export Gemini access to files in Drive: Admins can access Gemini audit logs using the [Reports API](https://developers.google.com/workspace/admin/reports/v1/appendix/activity/gemini-in-workspace-apps), which indicate instances when Gemini accessed a Drive file to fulfill a user query. These logs are also available in the [security investigation tool](https://support.google.com/a/answer/7575955?hl=en&sjid=16040906469908561115-NC), enabling Admins to query, investigate, and export Gemini access to Drive log data.
- [Use Google Vault to investigate conversations in the Gemini app](https://support.google.com/vault/answer/15695746): For eDiscovery purposes, administrators can leverage Vault to search and export relevant prompts and responses from the Gemini app.
- [Use the Data Export tool to export organizational data](https://support.google.com/a/answer/14339894?hl=en&ref_topic=14104519&sjid=15502736214237378009-NC): Organizations retain full control over their data in Workspace. Administrators can export their [Gemini app](https://support.google.com/a/answer/14817835?sjid=14448788510783536230-NC) and [NotebookLM](https://support.google.com/a/answer/16054396) data for archive or backup purposes using the Data Export tool. Admins are able to [manage](https://support.google.com/a/answer/15706919?hl=en&src=supportwidget0&authuser=0#zippy=%2Chow-long-are-prompts-saved) the duration for how long prompts and responses are saved in the Gemini app, enabling this content to be exported (this setting is not available for Gemini in Workspace apps, which does not save prompts or responses).

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Screenshot_2025-07-11_10.33.29_AM.max-2000x2000.png](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Screenshot_2025-07-11_10.33.29_AM.max-2000x2000.png)

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Screenshot_2025-07-11_10.33.29_AM.max-2000x2000.png](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Screenshot_2025-07-11_10.33.29_AM.max-2000x2000.png)

A call using the Reports API to query recent Gemini in Workspace usage activity for a given user.

### Deploying AI with flexibility

Workspace offers granular controls to manage user access, allowing administrators to define who can leverage these powerful and robust AI capabilities.

- [Manage Gemini for your organization](https://support.google.com/a/topic/15995054?hl=en&ref_topic=13853688&sjid=15502736214237378009-NC): Admins can enable or disable Gemini features across Workspace applications like Gmail, Google Drive, and Google Docs. This allows for precise access control based on users, groups, or organizational units.
- [Manage access to the Gemini app](https://support.google.com/a/answer/14571493?hl=en&ref_topic=15995054&sjid=15502736214237378009-NC): Admins have the flexibility to grant or restrict user access to the standalone Gemini app based on specific users, groups, or organizational units.
- [Turn Workspace apps in Gemini on or off](https://support.google.com/a/answer/15293691?hl=en&sjid=16230191034105272886-NA): Admins can control whether users can utilize the Workspace extension within the Gemini app, which enables the Gemini app to leverage Workspace data.
- [Turn NotebookLM on or off](https://support.google.com/a/answer/15239506): Similar to Gemini, admins can manage user access to NotebookLM and NotebookLM Plus across various users, groups, or organizational units.

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Screenshot_2025-07-11_10.28.02_AM.max-2000x2000.png](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Screenshot_2025-07-11_10.28.02_AM.max-2000x2000.png)

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Screenshot_2025-07-11_10.28.02_AM.max-2000x2000.png](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Screenshot_2025-07-11_10.28.02_AM.max-2000x2000.png)

Admin console UI with granular settings to restrict user access of Gemini in Workspace to specific Workspace apps for users, groups, and organizational units.

### Securing access points

Beyond user-level access, organizations can implement device-specific policies to further secure Gemini usage.

- [Control access to apps based on user and device context](https://support.google.com/a/answer/9261439?hl=en&sjid=16230191034105272886-NA): Administrators can assign context-aware access (CAA) policies to limit access to the Gemini app and NotebookLM on compliant devices based on factors such as user identity, device security status, IP address, and geographical location.
- [Apply endpoint DLP policies to the Gemini app](https://support.google.com/a/answer/10104358): [Chrome Enterprise Premium](https://chromeenterprise.google/products/chrome-enterprise-premium/) enables robust data loss prevention (DLP) that extend to the Chrome browser, including the Gemini app and NotebookLM. This allows for more granular control over actions including copy and paste, printing, and uploads/downloads of sensitive data into the Chrome browser. It also offers personally identifiable information (PII) masking and screenshot protection, with all relevant activities logged for audit.

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Screenshot_2025-07-11_10.29.56_AM.max-1700x1700.png](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Screenshot_2025-07-11_10.29.56_AM.max-1700x1700.png)

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Screenshot_2025-07-11_10.29.56_AM.max-1700x1700.png](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Screenshot_2025-07-11_10.29.56_AM.max-1700x1700.png)

Admin console UI with granular access settings for devices and OS, restricting user access levels for the Gemini app to a specific organizational unit.

*“We became the first major financial institution in Canada to empower all of our team members with Google AI in Workspace. In the pilot program, we saw that Google AI offered significant time savings and productivity gains for team members, allowing them to automate routine tasks, access information quickly, and collaborate more effectively, all while ensuring data is secure and trustworthy. Today, we’re confident this technology will help all of our team members succeed in their roles, while delivering even more exceptional experiences to our more than 820,000 clients."***- John Tarnowski, Chief Client Experience and Technology Officer, ATB Financial**

By implementing these robust security controls, organizations can confidently embrace the productivity benefits of Gemini in Workspace while helping to maintain a strong security posture and ensuring data protection.

## 51. Enterprise-ready, secure AI

**Source:** https://workspace.google.com/security/ai-privacy?hl=en

# Enterprise-ready, secure AI

Source: https://workspace.google.com/security/ai-privacy?hl=en

# Enterprise-ready, secure AI

Google Workspace with Gemini empowers teams to achieve more and helps keep data confidential, compliant, and secure.

[Contact sales](https://workspace.google.com/contact/?source=gafb-ai_privacy-hero-en)[Download the whitepaper](https://workspace.google.com/learning/content/gemini-privacy-security-compliance-whitepaper?utm_source=wgc&utm_medium=et&utm_campaign=FY25Q2-security-and-privacy-lp-launch&utm_term=-)

![The Google logo on a security shield surrounded by icons for biometrics, passkeys, and online safety and a Gemini prompt bar in the foreground.](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/6_nLF9K9H3xWtddmXh9PEf9Qannr0V-uMPIqNA6-GNNoM05QUy108UuMEgPVaQqBlZOJDlAwb1ePmSxggaCon934KCIKgBy6Ns4=e365-pa-nu-rw-w360)

### What is generative AI privacy and security?

Generative AI has become an indispensable tool, integrated into daily life to enhance productivity, foster creativity, and provide convenience. Whether itâs planning a vacation, researching a topic, writing a marketing brief, or staying connected with a global team â [generative AI](https://workspace.google.com/solutions/ai/) can be a personal creative assistant to spark ideas and help get things done. To accomplish that, it may access various data sources, and not all tools offer the same AI safety and security controls. With its rapid adoption and growing importance, organizations are rightly concerned about potential data security and privacy risks. Evaluating generative AIâs infrastructure, data governance policies, and security controls to protect sensitive data is a must.

### Keep business data confidential, secure, and compliant in Google Workspace with Gemini

![A screenshot of the Gemini chat interface where the response is marked with a blue shield and padlock icon for privacy.](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/q7J0NJrsLZh-clJ2TliElkfPbJsqZ6JNelYZFcSbOe3VsEU2nubvATCgYZydegmJ3i-agmOL1MgSZRSHIkZbuyMhiq4z200GyvWd=e365-pa-nu-rw-w370)

### Confidential

Your data, including personal and business data in products such as Gmail, is not reviewed by humans or used for [generative AI model training](https://support.google.com/a/answer/15706919) outside your domain without permission.

![A screenshot of the Gemini chat interface where Gemini has removed a suspicious link from an email it was summarizing.](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/KATI-V8phq1mX0ViupIYSfzyhyaMyLjdv39dWVg6Ns1lvxnJlJw82GNWjXukXWBSZ92G32jaYJE6XPrO9cV0757dULW22TpvGgA=e365-pa-nu-rw-w370)

### Secure

Gemini is built with indirect prompt injection [defenses](https://security.googleblog.com/2025/06/mitigating-prompt-injection-attacks.html) and comes with granular DLP [controls](https://workspace.google.com/blog/ai-and-machine-learning/enterprise-security-controls-google-workspace-gemini) to help protect sensitive content.

![A checklist of Geminiâs security certifications, including HIPAA, SOC, and ISO.](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/qOKVWFlqL2KjvtUttDK7vXSBnlcfWeHnHgJdPyBi3N7et2cVurOY3PEgANwMUCIOOqzlljbBmiaQ0y80SCGboma4leEzQfFGtvZE=e365-pa-nu-rw-w370)

### Compliant

Gemini has received security and privacy [certifications](https://support.google.com/a/answer/15706919#what-controls&audit-logs&data-processing-and-storage&threat-protection&scope-of-agreements&compliance-certifications&HIPAA&FedRAMP&EU-AI-act&DPIA&GDPR), including ISO 42001, BSI C5 and FedRAMP High, and can help meet HIPAA requirements.

![A screenshot of the Gemini chat interface where the response is marked with a blue shield and padlock icon for privacy.](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/q7J0NJrsLZh-clJ2TliElkfPbJsqZ6JNelYZFcSbOe3VsEU2nubvATCgYZydegmJ3i-agmOL1MgSZRSHIkZbuyMhiq4z200GyvWd=e365-pa-nu-rw-w370)

### Confidential

Your data, including personal and business data in products such as Gmail, is not reviewed by humans or used for [generative AI model training](https://support.google.com/a/answer/15706919) outside your domain without permission.

![A screenshot of the Gemini chat interface where Gemini has removed a suspicious link from an email it was summarizing.](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/KATI-V8phq1mX0ViupIYSfzyhyaMyLjdv39dWVg6Ns1lvxnJlJw82GNWjXukXWBSZ92G32jaYJE6XPrO9cV0757dULW22TpvGgA=e365-pa-nu-rw-w370)

### Secure

Gemini is built with indirect prompt injection [defenses](https://security.googleblog.com/2025/06/mitigating-prompt-injection-attacks.html) and comes with granular DLP [controls](https://workspace.google.com/blog/ai-and-machine-learning/enterprise-security-controls-google-workspace-gemini) to help protect sensitive content.

![A checklist of Geminiâs security certifications, including HIPAA, SOC, and ISO.](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/qOKVWFlqL2KjvtUttDK7vXSBnlcfWeHnHgJdPyBi3N7et2cVurOY3PEgANwMUCIOOqzlljbBmiaQ0y80SCGboma4leEzQfFGtvZE=e365-pa-nu-rw-w370)

### Compliant

Gemini has received security and privacy [certifications](https://support.google.com/a/answer/15706919#what-controls&audit-logs&data-processing-and-storage&threat-protection&scope-of-agreements&compliance-certifications&HIPAA&FedRAMP&EU-AI-act&DPIA&GDPR), including ISO 42001, BSI C5 and FedRAMP High, and can help meet HIPAA requirements.

](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/TOTnyrrZyYiFHfqlXcgFAZAbsCT9t1Z9hhGPcBIzYq-r5O5HJ51Kvjybr4ZbfbSfw6ZSp3CRZTOOaaxa3E5pqgDbzXkZWKjMCspI=e365-pa-nu-rw-w684)

](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/bWE3-tnQbnhV6oNjL49DmCrX4tGgC714IpkgQWVrFzJdVaWFaZrP4xSex-Kroee0zEUHru6AXncdlM4-eaH1p1HDpXBuy9Hjbxak=e365-pa-nu-rw-w304)

## Equifax securely provides Gemini to a global workforce, saving teams hours per day

Equifax, a leading credit bureau with a global footprint, launched Google Workspace with Gemini, boosting productivity across its organization.

![Equifax embraces secure generative AI with Gemini YouTube video thumbnail ](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/75UuKcONR30qyw49hnCG6pQVm86gQoxpYBXBdRF2zCAiQcIIFW8-WtiPTvq8bsRquqFNQlfn21i1r1BmK5ew-CE2MC6kJQmVbk0=e365-pa-nu-rw-w304)

![The sovereign AI pyramid, showing six layers of Google security: client-side encryption, context-aware access, access controls, local data storage, data regions, and zero-trust secure architecture.](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/ptM_IKg1LEmDfbIMXkbG-FU5QsS3q7pSxXRYruMHvTJTyB744IG1uMWyi1T3XLZtkD7x3EzC_5wOj5a1txSvSu_fDDZibB_5ofg=e365-pa-nu-rw-w304)

## Gemini in Google Workspace delivers sovereign AI

Customers can control where their data is stored and processed (EU or US). Customers can also limit data access to Google-support personnel in a specific region to retain sovereign control over their data.

### Mitigating prompt injection attacks with a layered defense strategy

[Read the blog](https://security.googleblog.com/2025/06/mitigating-prompt-injection-attacks.html )

## Frequently asked questions

Expand all

- While generative AI tools are enabling employees to reach new levels of productivity and creativity, it is critical to select a solution that prioritizes AI safety and security. Organizations also need to be intentional about their policies, including limiting the widespread use of âshadowâ AI tools that may increase the risk of data loss.

  By leveraging an integrated generative AI assistant that respects user access controls and provides granular management settings, administrators can help safeguard data and reduce the risk of data loss. Google Workspace with Gemini has a robust set of built-in threat defenses, data protections, and compliance [controls](https://workspace.google.com/blog/ai-and-machine-learning/enterprise-security-controls-google-workspace-gemini), allowing organizations to get the most out of generative AI without making tradeoffs to their security posture.
- With Workspace, files are securely stored and automatically versioned in a single place - Google Drive. This makes it easier for IT teams to protect business data and for Gemini to reference one single version of the document based on applied user permissions. Gemini can only retrieve data that the user has permissions to access, which minimizes additional overhead in terms of managing access and costs for separate tools with access to these data sources.

  Security capabilities, such as AI classification, can help identify, manage, and protect data at scale by leveraging labels and data loss prevention (DLP) rules automatically. With content restrictions, such as Information Rights Management (disable downloading, copying, or printing) and client-side encryption, admins can also help restrict Geminiâs access to sensitive data. Take a closer look at available data security controls [here](https://workspace.google.com/blog/ai-and-machine-learning/enterprise-security-controls-google-workspace-gemini).
- Using the capabilities of Google Cloud Data Boundary as a foundation, Gemini in Workspace apps offers authoritative sovereign controls for customer data. Customers can confine Gemini in Workspace data processing to the US or EU. For digital resilience and survivability, customers can also store an independent copy of their Gemini in Workspace apps data in any country of their choice using local data storage.

  Furthermore, for their most critical data, customers can use client-side encryption, a unique technical control that provides end-to-end encryption and places the keys solely in the control of Workspace customers to help prevent all unauthorized access, even by Google, Gemini, and other generative AI assistants in an authoritative manner.
- Google does not use customersâ Workspace data to train or improve the underlying generative AI and large language models (LLMs) that power Gemini, Search, and other systems outside of Workspace without permission. This has also been previously shared in a [blog post](https://workspace.google.com/blog/identity-and-security/protecting-your-data-era-generative-ai) on how Google protects Workspace data and maintains these [privacy commitments](https://cloud.google.com/privacy) in the era of generative AI.
- Google has taken a layered security approach introducing security measures designed for each stage of the prompt lifecycle. From Gemini 2.5 model hardening, to purpose-built machine learning (ML) models detecting malicious instructions, to system-level safeguards, we are meaningfully elevating the difficulty, expense, and complexity faced by an attacker. This approach compels adversaries to resort to methods that are either more easily identified or demand greater resources.

  Our model training with adversarial data significantly enhanced our defenses against indirect prompt injection attacks in Gemini 2.5 models . This inherent model resilience is augmented with additional defenses that we built directly into Gemini. Learn more about these [additional defenses](https://security.googleblog.com/2025/06/mitigating-prompt-injection-attacks.html).
- Google Workspace with Gemini is [enterprise-ready](https://workspace.google.com/blog/ai-and-machine-learning/enterprise-security-controls-google-workspace-gemini) and comes with granular user access and data security controls, helping admins safely and securely deploy AI tools. Check out our step-by-step [AI adoption guidelines](https://support.google.com/a/answer/15905903) and [whitepaper](https://services.google.com/fh/files/misc/2025_02_adopting_google_workspace_with_gemini_whitepaper.pdf) for further details, find additional information in the [Generative AI in Google Workspace Privacy Hub](https://support.google.com/a/answer/15706919), and get started with a no-cost [trial](https://workspace.google.com/business/signup/welcome?source=gafb-ai_privacy-body-en&hl=en&ga_region=amer&ga_country=us&ga_lang=en).

## 52. Enterprise-ready, secure AI

**Source:** https://workspace.google.com/intl/en_uk/security/ai-privacy?hl=en

# Enterprise-ready, secure AI

Source: https://workspace.google.com/intl/en_uk/security/ai-privacy?hl=en

# Enterprise-ready, secure AI

Google Workspace with Gemini empowers teams to achieve more and helps keep data confidential, compliant and secure.

[Contact sales](https://workspace.google.com/intl/en_uk/contact/?source=gafb-ai_privacy-hero-en-GB)[Download the white paper](https://workspace.google.com/learning/content/gemini-privacy-security-compliance-whitepaper?utm_source=wgc&utm_medium=et&utm_campaign=FY25Q2-security-and-privacy-lp-launch&utm_term=-&hl=en-GB)

![The Google logo on a security shield surrounded by icons for biometrics, passkeys and online safety, and a Gemini prompt bar in the foreground.](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/6_nLF9K9H3xWtddmXh9PEf9Qannr0V-uMPIqNA6-GNNoM05QUy108UuMEgPVaQqBlZOJDlAwb1ePmSxggaCon934KCIKgBy6Ns4=e365-pa-nu-rw-w360)

### What is generative AI privacy and security?

Generative AI has become an indispensable tool, integrated into daily life to enhance productivity, foster creativity and provide convenience. Whether it's planning a holiday, researching a topic, writing a marketing brief or staying connected with a global team â [generative AI](https://workspace.google.com/intl/en_uk/solutions/ai/) can be a personal creative assistant to spark ideas and help get things done. To accomplish that, it may access various data sources, and not all tools offer the same AI safety and security controls. With its rapid adoption and growing importance, organisations are rightly concerned about potential data security and privacy risks. Evaluating generative AI's infrastructure, data governance policies and security controls to protect sensitive data is a must.

### Keep business data confidential, secure and compliant in Google Workspace with Gemini

![A screenshot of the Gemini chat interface where the response is marked with a blue shield and padlock icon for privacy.](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/q7J0NJrsLZh-clJ2TliElkfPbJsqZ6JNelYZFcSbOe3VsEU2nubvATCgYZydegmJ3i-agmOL1MgSZRSHIkZbuyMhiq4z200GyvWd=e365-pa-nu-rw-w370)

### Confidential

Your data, including personal and business data in products such as Gmail, is not reviewed by humans or used for [generative AI model training](https://support.google.com/a/answer/15706919?hl=en-GB) outside your domain without permission.

![A screenshot of the Gemini Chat interface where Gemini has removed a suspicious link from an email that it was summarising.](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/KATI-V8phq1mX0ViupIYSfzyhyaMyLjdv39dWVg6Ns1lvxnJlJw82GNWjXukXWBSZ92G32jaYJE6XPrO9cV0757dULW22TpvGgA=e365-pa-nu-rw-w370)

### Secure

Gemini is built with indirect prompt injection [defences](https://security.googleblog.com/2025/06/mitigating-prompt-injection-attacks.html) and comes with granular DLP [controls](https://workspace.google.com/blog/ai-and-machine-learning/enterprise-security-controls-google-workspace-gemini?hl=en-GB) to help protect sensitive content.

![A checklist of Gemini's security certifications, including HIPAA, SOC and ISO.](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/qOKVWFlqL2KjvtUttDK7vXSBnlcfWeHnHgJdPyBi3N7et2cVurOY3PEgANwMUCIOOqzlljbBmiaQ0y80SCGboma4leEzQfFGtvZE=e365-pa-nu-rw-w370)

### Compliant

Gemini has received security and privacy [certifications](https://support.google.com/a/answer/15706919?hl=en-GB#what-controls&audit-logs&data-processing-and-storage&threat-protection&scope-of-agreements&compliance-certifications&HIPAA&FedRAMP&EU-AI-act&DPIA&GDPR), including ISO 42001, BSI C5 and FedRAMP High, and can help meet HIPAA requirements.

![A screenshot of the Gemini chat interface where the response is marked with a blue shield and padlock icon for privacy.](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/q7J0NJrsLZh-clJ2TliElkfPbJsqZ6JNelYZFcSbOe3VsEU2nubvATCgYZydegmJ3i-agmOL1MgSZRSHIkZbuyMhiq4z200GyvWd=e365-pa-nu-rw-w370)

### Confidential

Your data, including personal and business data in products such as Gmail, is not reviewed by humans or used for [generative AI model training](https://support.google.com/a/answer/15706919?hl=en-GB) outside your domain without permission.

![A screenshot of the Gemini Chat interface where Gemini has removed a suspicious link from an email that it was summarising.](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/KATI-V8phq1mX0ViupIYSfzyhyaMyLjdv39dWVg6Ns1lvxnJlJw82GNWjXukXWBSZ92G32jaYJE6XPrO9cV0757dULW22TpvGgA=e365-pa-nu-rw-w370)

### Secure

Gemini is built with indirect prompt injection [defences](https://security.googleblog.com/2025/06/mitigating-prompt-injection-attacks.html) and comes with granular DLP [controls](https://workspace.google.com/blog/ai-and-machine-learning/enterprise-security-controls-google-workspace-gemini?hl=en-GB) to help protect sensitive content.

![A checklist of Gemini's security certifications, including HIPAA, SOC and ISO.](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/qOKVWFlqL2KjvtUttDK7vXSBnlcfWeHnHgJdPyBi3N7et2cVurOY3PEgANwMUCIOOqzlljbBmiaQ0y80SCGboma4leEzQfFGtvZE=e365-pa-nu-rw-w370)

### Compliant

Gemini has received security and privacy [certifications](https://support.google.com/a/answer/15706919?hl=en-GB#what-controls&audit-logs&data-processing-and-storage&threat-protection&scope-of-agreements&compliance-certifications&HIPAA&FedRAMP&EU-AI-act&DPIA&GDPR), including ISO 42001, BSI C5 and FedRAMP High, and can help meet HIPAA requirements.

### How to minimise risks with generative AI tools

![A screenshot of a rule being created to disable the download, print and copy options for all collaborators in a document.
](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/TOTnyrrZyYiFHfqlXcgFAZAbsCT9t1Z9hhGPcBIzYq-r5O5HJ51Kvjybr4ZbfbSfw6ZSp3CRZTOOaaxa3E5pqgDbzXkZWKjMCspI=e365-pa-nu-rw-w684)

![A screenshot of Gemini activity in Google Workspace being logged and exported.](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/VZqDUhoN6YX1mHQgqeqx5LsHDeUkJEALbMEAPz1rnSaUVEA_E_N0LhPDPQakK7JjNh6KqPhlFX22oaIVaUxNzzMRtCU9DU2xFOE=e365-pa-nu-rw-w684)

![A screenshot showing the controls for Gemini in Google Workspace, with access enabled for Drive, Docs and Meet, but disabled for Gmail.](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/D52RfKK6THjBVujOmZSHgxj35jIHJldlj00vipp-bnK0m5uSQDUMtxztETn3vFDRZPLT2gmwL3_sau-HB2obc-Bl1DzNyvDhCAsZ=e365-pa-nu-rw-w684)

![A screenshot of access policies being applied to the Gemini app, where 'Company-approved devices' has been set to 'Monitor'.](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/eZo-bBJzAzk7rTHHzArfJ0aNvT-T_huqzplnkkoNf_Ek9xyDdt85XTAYej7OpV4b6f9zJbYiQsGft-Zbggbp2FkGMgiQ6ZKZtA=e365-pa-nu-rw-w684)

### Protecting your data

Gemini integrates with existing data security measures in Workspace to help prevent unauthorised data access and exfiltration.

![A screenshot of a rule being created to disable the download, print and copy options for all collaborators in a document.
](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/bWE3-tnQbnhV6oNjL49DmCrX4tGgC714IpkgQWVrFzJdVaWFaZrP4xSex-Kroee0zEUHru6AXncdlM4-eaH1p1HDpXBuy9Hjbxak=e365-pa-nu-rw-w304)

### Understanding AI usage

Workspace provides comprehensive logging and exporting capabilities for Gemini activity.

![A screenshot of Gemini activity in Google Workspace being logged and exported.](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/obTTemXCvF-GqBSxNJNeg93xmPXvOXoxddflQZ183EENmbgutiBllf7MQv0i923N8P_HUN2QR6mFgWaRyFW_91NIV1vYxwYdB9nplw=e365-pa-nu-rw-w304)

### Deploying AI with flexibility

Workspace offers granular controls to manage user access, allowing administrators to define who can leverage these powerful and robust AI capabilities.

![A screenshot showing the controls for Gemini in Google Workspace, with access enabled for Drive, Docs and Meet, but disabled for Gmail.](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/Jbn9x1GmY0CyL-787bdiswVJvmtG3m82E4lNKPN9ncKSKdBL4azvDGuNvc-n7VGdZH4MV3d0YUvIJIvO1tfOnIkOAWYm9eK2crk=e365-pa-nu-rw-w304)

### Securing access points

Organisations can implement device-specific access policies to further secure Gemini usage.

![A screenshot of access policies being applied to the Gemini app, where 'Company-approved devices' has been set to 'Monitor'.](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/nEKnOewXUp97DwHU2Gi85dFTZL08THNAqVGnqomKyFr_fq3QwRG687CPikIpVsTsLJC74PaHYhHE9N90sML03pZ_S-XZCofx26QHnA=e365-pa-nu-rw-w304)

## Equifax securely provides Gemini to a global workforce, saving teams hours per day

Equifax, a leading credit bureau with a global footprint, launched Google Workspace with Gemini, boosting productivity across its organisation.

![Equifax embraces secure generative AI with Gemini YouTube video thumbnail ](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/75UuKcONR30qyw49hnCG6pQVm86gQoxpYBXBdRF2zCAiQcIIFW8-WtiPTvq8bsRquqFNQlfn21i1r1BmK5ew-CE2MC6kJQmVbk0=e365-pa-nu-rw-w304)

![The sovereign AI pyramid, showing six layers of Google security: client-side encryption, context-aware access, access controls, local data storage, data regions and zero-trust secure architecture.](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/ptM_IKg1LEmDfbIMXkbG-FU5QsS3q7pSxXRYruMHvTJTyB744IG1uMWyi1T3XLZtkD7x3EzC_5wOj5a1txSvSu_fDDZibB_5ofg=e365-pa-nu-rw-w304)

## Gemini in Google Workspace delivers sovereign AI

Customers can control where their data is stored and processed (EU or US). Customers can also limit data access to Google-support personnel in a specific region to retain sovereign control over their data.

### Customer testimonials

![Air Liquid logo ](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-workspace-assets/uploads/7uffzv9dk4sn-1Qrc6AgDFatnFmnVCtl0td-bea31668e627caf602310d00bd3f4157-SEO_Gemini_Privacy___Compliance_Logo_Air_Liquide.svg)

> Gemini is in a really unique place in being able to securely access all of our documentation while maintaining the security posture that we have built up over a decade of using Workspace. It's not something that we can easily replicate in any other tool.

Jeremy Gibbons, Digital and IT CTO, Air Liquide

![ATB Financial logo ](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-workspace-assets/uploads/7uffzv9dk4sn-5yUBMLKtQ7bnnmuO2hjPBE-eb1d952b196fbcb0c035c00348c6b1a4-SEO_Gemini_Privacy___Compliance_Logo_ATB_Financial.svg)

> Gemini is an enterprise-grade application that respects our data and infrastructure security while at the same time allowing us to safely experiment with new gen AI capabilities.

Katie Peperkorn, AVP, Generative AI, AI Platforms & Virtual Agents, ATB Financials

[Learn more](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=wfDJgOs8fBQ&t=1711s)

![Flashpoint logo ](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-workspace-assets/uploads/7uffzv9dk4sn-1X7oddsaDjhuTh2H9SC6hH-53227a35bed41a2c81b0b59c4fde4045-SEO_Gemini_Privacy___Compliance_Logo_Flashpoint.svg)

> Gemini doesn't change how we trust Workspace with our data. Gemini doesn't train the model on our data, and features are enterprise-ready as soon as they are released, so our administrators have confidence that they can deploy Gemini in a secure manner.

Tyler Predale, Director, IT Systems, Flashpoint

![Fullstory logo ](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-workspace-assets/uploads/7uffzv9dk4sn-1NG67r68IbAr6d8crrkdgx-a4bfd94020c7863ba32868b426d2a5a5-SEO_Gemini_Privacy___Compliance_Logo_Fullstory.svg)

> The security benefits of this really cannot be overstated. It's critical to be purposeful and find solutions from a vendor that meets your needs without compromising your security. Gemini has the same security posture already built in that we have existing with Google Workspace.

Forest Donovan, Senior Director of Strategic Alliances, Fullstory

Expand all

Organisations also need to be intentional about their policies, including limiting the widespread use of 'shadow' AI tools that may increase the risk of data loss.

  By leveraging an integrated generative AI assistant that respects user access controls and provides granular management settings, administrators can help safeguard data and reduce the risk of data loss. This makes it easier for IT teams to protect business data and for Gemini to reference one single version of the document based on applied user permissions. Gemini can only retrieve data that the user has permissions to access, which minimises additional overhead in terms of managing access and costs for separate tools with access to these data sources.

  Security capabilities, such as AI classification, can help identify, manage and protect data at scale by leveraging labels and data loss prevention (DLP) rules automatically. With content restrictions, such as information rights management (disable downloading, copying or printing) and client-side encryption, admins can also help restrict Gemini's access to sensitive data. Customers can confine Gemini in Workspace data processing to the US or EU. For digital resilience and survivability, customers can also store an independent copy of their Gemini in Workspace apps data in any country of their choice using local data storage.

- Google has taken a layered security approach, introducing security measures designed for each stage of the prompt lifecycle. From Gemini 2.5 model hardening, to purpose-built machine learning (ML) models detecting malicious instructions, to system-level safeguards, we are meaningfully elevating the difficulty, expense and complexity faced by an attacker. This approach compels adversaries to resort to methods that are either more easily identified or demand greater resources.

  Our model training with adversarial data significantly enhanced our defences against indirect prompt injection attacks in Gemini 2.5 models. This inherent model resilience is augmented with additional defences that we built directly into Gemini. Learn more about these [additional defences](https://security.googleblog.com/2025/06/mitigating-prompt-injection-attacks.html).
- Google Workspace with Gemini is [enterprise-ready](https://workspace.google.com/blog/ai-and-machine-learning/enterprise-security-controls-google-workspace-gemini?hl=en-GB) and comes with granular user access and data security controls, helping admins safely and securely deploy AI tools. Check out our step-by-step [AI adoption guidelines](https://support.google.com/a/answer/15905903?hl=en-GB) and [white paper](https://services.google.com/fh/files/misc/2025_02_adopting_google_workspace_with_gemini_whitepaper.pdf) for further details, find additional information in the [Generative AI in Google Workspace Privacy Hub](https://support.google.com/a/answer/15706919?hl=en-GB) and get started with a no-cost [trial](https://workspace.google.com/business/signup/welcome?source=gafb-ai_privacy-body-en-GB&hl=en-GB&ga_region=emea&ga_country=uk&ga_lang=en).

## 53. Enterprise-ready, secure AI

**Source:** https://workspace.google.com/intl/en_ca/security/ai-privacy?hl=en

# Enterprise-ready, secure AI

Source: https://workspace.google.com/intl/en_ca/security/ai-privacy?hl=en

# Enterprise-ready, secure AI

Google Workspace with Gemini empowers teams to achieve more and helps keep data confidential, compliant, and secure.

[Contact sales](https://workspace.google.com/intl/en_ca/contact/?source=gafb-ai_privacy-hero-en-CA)[Download the whitepaper](https://workspace.google.com/learning/content/gemini-privacy-security-compliance-whitepaper?utm_source=wgc&utm_medium=et&utm_campaign=FY25Q2-security-and-privacy-lp-launch&utm_term=-&hl=en-CA)

![The Google logo on a security shield surrounded by icons for biometrics, passkeys, and online safety and a Gemini prompt bar in the foreground.](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/6_nLF9K9H3xWtddmXh9PEf9Qannr0V-uMPIqNA6-GNNoM05QUy108UuMEgPVaQqBlZOJDlAwb1ePmSxggaCon934KCIKgBy6Ns4=e365-pa-nu-rw-w360)

### What is generative AI privacy and security?

Generative AI has become an indispensable tool, integrated into daily life to enhance productivity, foster creativity, and provide convenience. Whether itâs planning a vacation, researching a topic, writing a marketing brief, or staying connected with a global team â [generative AI](https://workspace.google.com/intl/en_ca/solutions/ai/) can be a personal creative assistant to spark ideas and help get things done. To accomplish that, it may access various data sources, and not all tools offer the same AI safety and security controls. With its rapid adoption and growing importance, organizations are rightly concerned about potential data security and privacy risks. Evaluating generative AIâs infrastructure, data governance policies, and security controls to protect sensitive data is a must.

### Keep business data confidential, secure, and compliant in Google Workspace with Gemini

![A screenshot of the Gemini chat interface where the response is marked with a blue shield and padlock icon for privacy.](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/q7J0NJrsLZh-clJ2TliElkfPbJsqZ6JNelYZFcSbOe3VsEU2nubvATCgYZydegmJ3i-agmOL1MgSZRSHIkZbuyMhiq4z200GyvWd=e365-pa-nu-rw-w370)

### Confidential

Your data, including personal and business data in products such as Gmail, is not reviewed by humans or used for [generative AI model training](https://support.google.com/a/answer/15706919?hl=en-CA) outside your domain without permission.

![A screenshot of the Gemini chat interface where Gemini has removed a suspicious link from an email it was summarizing.](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/KATI-V8phq1mX0ViupIYSfzyhyaMyLjdv39dWVg6Ns1lvxnJlJw82GNWjXukXWBSZ92G32jaYJE6XPrO9cV0757dULW22TpvGgA=e365-pa-nu-rw-w370)

### Secure

Gemini is built with indirect prompt injection [defenses](https://security.googleblog.com/2025/06/mitigating-prompt-injection-attacks.html) and comes with granular DLP [controls](https://workspace.google.com/blog/ai-and-machine-learning/enterprise-security-controls-google-workspace-gemini?hl=en-CA) to help protect sensitive content.

![A checklist of Geminiâs security certifications, including HIPAA, SOC, and ISO.](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/qOKVWFlqL2KjvtUttDK7vXSBnlcfWeHnHgJdPyBi3N7et2cVurOY3PEgANwMUCIOOqzlljbBmiaQ0y80SCGboma4leEzQfFGtvZE=e365-pa-nu-rw-w370)

### Compliant

Gemini has received security and privacy [certifications](https://support.google.com/a/answer/15706919?hl=en-CA#what-controls&audit-logs&data-processing-and-storage&threat-protection&scope-of-agreements&compliance-certifications&HIPAA&FedRAMP&EU-AI-act&DPIA&GDPR), including ISO 42001, BSI C5 and FedRAMP High, and can help meet HIPAA requirements.

![A screenshot of the Gemini chat interface where the response is marked with a blue shield and padlock icon for privacy.](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/q7J0NJrsLZh-clJ2TliElkfPbJsqZ6JNelYZFcSbOe3VsEU2nubvATCgYZydegmJ3i-agmOL1MgSZRSHIkZbuyMhiq4z200GyvWd=e365-pa-nu-rw-w370)

### Confidential

Your data, including personal and business data in products such as Gmail, is not reviewed by humans or used for [generative AI model training](https://support.google.com/a/answer/15706919?hl=en-CA) outside your domain without permission.

![A screenshot of the Gemini chat interface where Gemini has removed a suspicious link from an email it was summarizing.](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/KATI-V8phq1mX0ViupIYSfzyhyaMyLjdv39dWVg6Ns1lvxnJlJw82GNWjXukXWBSZ92G32jaYJE6XPrO9cV0757dULW22TpvGgA=e365-pa-nu-rw-w370)

### Secure

Gemini is built with indirect prompt injection [defenses](https://security.googleblog.com/2025/06/mitigating-prompt-injection-attacks.html) and comes with granular DLP [controls](https://workspace.google.com/blog/ai-and-machine-learning/enterprise-security-controls-google-workspace-gemini?hl=en-CA) to help protect sensitive content.

![A checklist of Geminiâs security certifications, including HIPAA, SOC, and ISO.](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/qOKVWFlqL2KjvtUttDK7vXSBnlcfWeHnHgJdPyBi3N7et2cVurOY3PEgANwMUCIOOqzlljbBmiaQ0y80SCGboma4leEzQfFGtvZE=e365-pa-nu-rw-w370)

### Compliant

Gemini has received security and privacy [certifications](https://support.google.com/a/answer/15706919?hl=en-CA#what-controls&audit-logs&data-processing-and-storage&threat-protection&scope-of-agreements&compliance-certifications&HIPAA&FedRAMP&EU-AI-act&DPIA&GDPR), including ISO 42001, BSI C5 and FedRAMP High, and can help meet HIPAA requirements.

### How to minimize risks with generative AI tools

![A screenshot of a rule being created to disable the download, print, and copy options for all collaborators in a document.
](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/TOTnyrrZyYiFHfqlXcgFAZAbsCT9t1Z9hhGPcBIzYq-r5O5HJ51Kvjybr4ZbfbSfw6ZSp3CRZTOOaaxa3E5pqgDbzXkZWKjMCspI=e365-pa-nu-rw-w684)

### Protecting your data

Gemini integrates with existing data security measures in Workspace to help prevent unauthorized data access and exfiltration.

![A screenshot of a rule being created to disable the download, print, and copy options for all collaborators in a document.
](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/bWE3-tnQbnhV6oNjL49DmCrX4tGgC714IpkgQWVrFzJdVaWFaZrP4xSex-Kroee0zEUHru6AXncdlM4-eaH1p1HDpXBuy9Hjbxak=e365-pa-nu-rw-w304)

![A screenshot of Gemini activity in Google Workspace being logged and exported.](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/obTTemXCvF-GqBSxNJNeg93xmPXvOXoxddflQZ183EENmbgutiBllf7MQv0i923N8P_HUN2QR6mFgWaRyFW_91NIV1vYxwYdB9nplw=e365-pa-nu-rw-w304)

Workspace offers granular controls to manage user access, allowing administrators to define who can leverage these powerful and robust AI capabilities.

![A screenshot showing the controls for Gemini in Google Workspace, with access enabled for Drive, Docs, and Meet, but disabled for Gmail.](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/Jbn9x1GmY0CyL-787bdiswVJvmtG3m82E4lNKPN9ncKSKdBL4azvDGuNvc-n7VGdZH4MV3d0YUvIJIvO1tfOnIkOAWYm9eK2crk=e365-pa-nu-rw-w304)

### Securing access points

Organizations can implement device-specific access policies to further secure Gemini usage.

![A screenshot of access policies being applied to the Gemini app, where 'Company Approved Devices' has been set to 'Monitor'.](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/nEKnOewXUp97DwHU2Gi85dFTZL08THNAqVGnqomKyFr_fq3QwRG687CPikIpVsTsLJC74PaHYhHE9N90sML03pZ_S-XZCofx26QHnA=e365-pa-nu-rw-w304)

## Equifax securely provides Gemini to a global workforce, saving teams hours per day

Equifax, a leading credit bureau with a global footprint, launched Google Workspace with Gemini, boosting productivity across its organization.

![Equifax embraces secure generative AI with Gemini YouTube video thumbnail ](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/75UuKcONR30qyw49hnCG6pQVm86gQoxpYBXBdRF2zCAiQcIIFW8-WtiPTvq8bsRquqFNQlfn21i1r1BmK5ew-CE2MC6kJQmVbk0=e365-pa-nu-rw-w304)

## Gemini in Google Workspace delivers sovereign AI

Customers can control where their data is stored and processed (EU or US). Customers can also limit data access to Google-support personnel in a specific region to retain sovereign control over their data.

## Frequently asked questions

Expand all

- While generative AI tools are enabling employees to reach new levels of productivity and creativity, it is critical to select a solution that prioritizes AI safety and security. Organizations also need to be intentional about their policies, including limiting the widespread use of âshadowâ AI tools that may increase the risk of data loss.

  By leveraging an integrated generative AI assistant that respects user access controls and provides granular management settings, administrators can help safeguard data and reduce the risk of data loss. Google Workspace with Gemini has a robust set of built-in threat defenses, data protections, and compliance [controls](https://workspace.google.com/blog/ai-and-machine-learning/enterprise-security-controls-google-workspace-gemini?hl=en-CA), allowing organizations to get the most out of generative AI without making tradeoffs to their security posture.
- With Workspace, files are securely stored and automatically versioned in a single place - Google Drive. This makes it easier for IT teams to protect business data and for Gemini to reference one single version of the document based on applied user permissions. Gemini can only retrieve data that the user has permissions to access, which minimizes additional overhead in terms of managing access and costs for separate tools with access to these data sources.

  Security capabilities, such as AI classification, can help identify, manage, and protect data at scale by leveraging labels and data loss prevention (DLP) rules automatically. With content restrictions, such as Information Rights Management (disable downloading, copying, or printing) and client-side encryption, admins can also help restrict Geminiâs access to sensitive data. Take a closer look at available data security controls [here](https://workspace.google.com/blog/ai-and-machine-learning/enterprise-security-controls-google-workspace-gemini?hl=en-CA).
- Using the capabilities of Google Cloud Data Boundary as a foundation, Gemini in Workspace apps offers authoritative sovereign controls for customer data. Customers can confine Gemini in Workspace data processing to the US or EU. For digital resilience and survivability, customers can also store an independent copy of their Gemini in Workspace apps data in any country of their choice using local data storage.

  Furthermore, for their most critical data, customers can use client-side encryption, a unique technical control that provides end-to-end encryption and places the keys solely in the control of Workspace customers to help prevent all unauthorized access, even by Google, Gemini, and other generative AI assistants in an authoritative manner.
- Google does not use customersâ Workspace data to train or improve the underlying generative AI and large language models (LLMs) that power Gemini, Search, and other systems outside of Workspace without permission. This has also been previously shared in a [blog post](https://workspace.google.com/blog/identity-and-security/protecting-your-data-era-generative-ai?hl=en-CA) on how Google protects Workspace data and maintains these [privacy commitments](https://cloud.google.com/privacy?hl=en-CA) in the era of generative AI.
- Google has taken a layered security approach introducing security measures designed for each stage of the prompt lifecycle. From Gemini 2.5 model hardening, to purpose-built machine learning (ML) models detecting malicious instructions, to system-level safeguards, we are meaningfully elevating the difficulty, expense, and complexity faced by an attacker. This approach compels adversaries to resort to methods that are either more easily identified or demand greater resources.

  Our model training with adversarial data significantly enhanced our defenses against indirect prompt injection attacks in Gemini 2.5 models . This inherent model resilience is augmented with additional defenses that we built directly into Gemini. Learn more about these [additional defenses](https://security.googleblog.com/2025/06/mitigating-prompt-injection-attacks.html).
- Google Workspace with Gemini is [enterprise-ready](https://workspace.google.com/blog/ai-and-machine-learning/enterprise-security-controls-google-workspace-gemini?hl=en-CA) and comes with granular user access and data security controls, helping admins safely and securely deploy AI tools. Check out our step-by-step [AI adoption guidelines](https://support.google.com/a/answer/15905903?hl=en-CA) and [whitepaper](https://services.google.com/fh/files/misc/2025_02_adopting_google_workspace_with_gemini_whitepaper.pdf) for further details, find additional information in the [Generative AI in Google Workspace Privacy Hub](https://support.google.com/a/answer/15706919?hl=en-CA), and get started with a no-cost [trial](https://workspace.google.com/business/signup/welcome?source=gafb-ai_privacy-body-en-CA&hl=en-CA&ga_region=amer&ga_country=ca&ga_lang=en).

![Google G in a shield logo ](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-workspace-assets/uploads/7uffzv9dk4sn-1enXq23B2dmOvZyIM99J4E-c71c90081ea25e2eed7074e5a96a8eaf-security-badge.svg)

## 54. Equifax embraces secure generative AI with Gemini

**Source:** https://workspace.google.com/blog/customer-stories/equifax-embraces-gemini-for-secure-innovation-across-business-units?hl=en

# Equifax embraces secure generative AI with Gemini

Source: https://workspace.google.com/blog/customer-stories/equifax-embraces-gemini-for-secure-innovation-across-business-units?hl=en

Customer Stories

# Equifax embraces secure generative AI with Gemini

June 24, 2025

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Google_Workspace_Blog_Hero_images_3.max-2500x2500.png](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Google_Workspace_Blog_Hero_images_3.max-2500x2500.png)

##### JK Krug

Vice President of Digital Employee Experience, Equifax

##### Google Workspace Newsletter

Keep up with the evolving future of work and collaboration with insights, trends, and product news.

[SIGN UP](https://workspace.google.com/?source=gafb-blog_article-body-en#newsletter)

“Gemini is not a quick fix — it’s a collaborative partner that can support our objectives, and it’s up to us to learn how to communicate with it.”

**Snapshot:** Global credit bureau Equifax adopted Gemini to enhance productivity while strengthening its security posture. As a result of its Gemini trial, Equifax found that:

- 97% of participants wanted to keep their Gemini licenses
- 90% of participants experienced an increase in work quality and quantity
- Employees from nearly all business units **saved more** **than one hour per day**

---

### The mission and the challenge

[Equifax](https://www.equifax.com/about-equifax/who-we-are/) delivers insights to its customers that power decisions to move people forward. Over the past seven years, the organization has invested $3 billion in [The Equifax Cloud™](https://www.equifax.com/about-equifax/equifax-cloud/), a top-tier global technology and security infrastructure that has changed nearly every facet of its infrastructure and continues to set Equifax apart in the industry.

During this transformation, we knew we needed a partner who could help enhance enterprise [communication and collaboration](https://workspace.google.com/blog/customer-stories/how-equifax-achieved-ruthless-collaboration-google-workspace?e=48754805) across its teams, from anywhere around the world, in a secure, cloud-native environment, which we were able to do with the adoption of [Google Workspace](https://workspace.google.com/intl/en_ca/)

Security was at the forefront of our enterprise’s most critical needs as we looked to implement a generative AI solution. During our search, we ran a platform “bake-off” and found that [Gemini](https://gemini.google.com/) met all our security needs. The solution inherits all our existing Workspace security settings to keep our data, prompts, and work within the tenant, giving us peace of mind to deploy generative AI across our enterprise safely

Here’s how we use Gemini today to foster innovation across teams while improving our security posture.

![Video Thumbnail](https://img.youtube.com/vi/6MWXXcGiLec/hqdefault.jpg)

Learn how Equifax securely deployed generative AI across its global organization with Gemini.

### The switch to Gemini

Our goal for using Gemini was to increase efficiency and productivity, freeing up time for end users to work on more impactful initiatives and projects. To better understand the tool’s ROI, Equifax worked with Google Workspace and [SADA](https://sada.com/), an Insight company and Google Premier Partner, to run a six-month trial with more than 1,500 participants and a waitlist of over 300 people. SADA created custom Gemini training agendas to dive into specific use cases for Equifax. The SADA team also ran demos to show how Gemini integrates across the Workspace suite. This made it easy to understand how Gemini can enhance every step of our workflows.

The journey of integrating Gemini wasn't just about adoption — it was about discovery. With the support of our Google Workspace Customer Success team, we built a value-driven framework to help with audience selection, survey design, and measurement so that Equifax could effectively evaluate the value of Gemini. By providing a structured way to assess the tool’s business impact, we unlocked a wealth of insights into the specific tasks where it was truly making a difference. This allowed us to understand how our team was experiencing the power of Gemini on a daily basis.

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/unnamed_7.max-600x600.jpg](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/unnamed_7.max-600x600.jpg)

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/unnamed_7.max-600x600.jpg](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/unnamed_7.max-600x600.jpg)

Global credit bureau Equifax uses Gemini within Workspace applications to create and collaborate.

After the trial, 97% of users asked to keep their Gemini licenses, 90% saw an increase in work quality and quantity, and employees from almost every business unit saved more than one hour per day by using the tool. That’s valuable time back in their schedules to focus on more important work. For example, our help desk representatives use Gemini to look deeper at the data to determine how to improve the service they provide our end users. Other teams use the “take notes for me” feature to create transcripts, summaries, and action items from [Google Meet](https://meet.google.com/) calls, putting all the details in one place so we can share with anyone who misses a meeting.

### Looking ahead

As we prepare for phase two of our Gemini deployment with Google Workspace and SADA, we’re taking what we learned from the trial to deliver the best possible resources to our teams. We are rolling out Gemini to our nearly 22,000 employees and contractors, focusing on the most requested training topics, like prompting and use of the side panel, and team-specific use cases, such as those in marketing and finance. SADA will facilitate open office hours, creating an open forum where employees can drop in and ask questions if they feel stuck.

We’re also excited to evaluate new Gemini-enabled tools as we look to expand our generative AI usage in creative ways. [NotebookLM](https://notebooklm.google/) has the ability to upscale our collaboration with Gemini and allow us to do more with our data, while [Google Vids](https://workspace.google.com/intl/en_ca/products/vids/) can empower us to generate engaging video content at scale. In our experience, once people start using Gemini, they simply don’t want to stop using it — and we’re confident that Gemini will continue to support our efficiency, productivity, and security goals.
